source_description: In an apartment living room, Ross picks up a striped bag from an armchair.
He hands the bag to Susan standing next to Carol. "Okay, here's his diaper bag," Ross says.
Susan takes the striped bag.
Ross hands Susan a stuffed animal and says, "his Mr. Winky."
Ross hands Susan a stuffed animal and says, "his Mr. Winky."
He points to a baby sitting in a carrier on another armchair. "And oh, him. Hi," he adds, waving at the baby. Carol holds a bag.
He points to a baby sitting in a carrier on another armchair. "And oh, him. Hi," he adds, waving at the baby. Carol holds a bag.
Ross lowers his hand.
Carol asks, "So, how'd everything go?" Ross, holding a small object, answers, "Oh, great. Great."
Ross raises his hands with open palms as he says, "There was a projectile throwing up incident, but he started it." Carol smiles and audience laughter is heard.
Ross raises his hands with open palms as he says, "There was a projectile throwing up incident, but he started it." Carol smiles and audience laughter is heard.
Ross raises his hands with open palms as he says, "There was a projectile throwing up incident, but he started it." Carol smiles and audience laughter is heard.

Susan steps back as the audience laughter fades.
Carol says, "We've got to go."
Ross leans toward the baby carrier and replies, "Okay."
"All right," Carol says.
Carol tells Ross, "I've got some news. It's about us."
Carol tells Ross, "I've got some news. It's about us."
Ross points between himself and Carol, asking, "Oh, you and me?"
Carol clarifies, gesturing to Susan, "No, Susan and me. The other us." Audience laughter is heard.
Carol clarifies, gesturing to Susan, "No, Susan and me. The other us." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross lowers his hand as the laughter fades.
Ross looks at Carol, his posture still.
Ross looks at Carol, his posture still.
Carol smiles and says, "We're getting married."
Ross stares at Carol in silence as audience laughter plays.
Ross stares at Carol in silence as audience laughter plays.
Ross's jaw drops slightly as his gaze remains fixed on Carol.
Ross's jaw drops slightly as his gaze remains fixed on Carol.
Ross's volume drops as he says, "As in, I now pronounce you wife and wife." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross's volume drops as he says, "As in, I now pronounce you wife and wife." Audience laughter is heard.
Carol maintains her gaze on Ross.
Carol says, "Anyway, we'd like you to come. But we totally understand if you don't want to."
Carol says, "Anyway, we'd like you to come. But we totally understand if you don't want to."
Carol says, "Anyway, we'd like you to come. But we totally understand if you don't want to."
Ross replies, his pitch flat, "Yeah, well, why wouldn't I want to come? I had fun at"
Ross replies, his pitch flat, "Yeah, well, why wouldn't I want to come? I had fun at"

Carol and Susan look at Ross in silence as the laughter fades.
Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "Look, I just thought that no, I mean, hey, why shouldn't I be happy for you?"
Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "Look, I just thought that no, I mean, hey, why shouldn't I be happy for you?"
Ross steps toward a folded baby seat.
Ross says, "What would it say about me if I couldn't revel in your joy? And I'm reveling, baby. Believe me." He grabs the frame of the folded seat and shakes it. Audience laughter plays.

Ross looks down and pulls his finger free from the seat frame as the laughter fades.
Carol asks, "Want us to go?"
Carol asks, "Want us to go?"
Ross nods, his facial muscles tense. The scene goes black as upbeat instrumental music begins.
